pundit.name: Clint Dempsey
pundit.take: **"Ghost Pepper" is rippin' through this pool like a Texas tornado, and "The Backpass Assassin" is lookin' like he's tryin' to put in a rain delay.** Devin's got Spain and USA lightin' up the scoreboard, and with Bosnia rollin' through Qatar like that, he's sittin' pretty at the top. Meanwhile, Gayden out here thinkin' draftin' Jordan was like scoopin' a secret weapon, but looks like he forgot to check if they even had a ticket to the dance. Turkey's 'bout to face the USA, and I'm callin' it now: "Ghost Pepper" ain't chillin' — he's scorchin' the turf and leavin' Gayden clutchin' his tactics board wonderin' what just hit him. Devin's takin' this one, no doubt, like he's already written the dateline in the stars. Grab some popcorn, y'all, this one's gonna be spicy.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin Secures Top Spot After Bosnia's Win Over Qatar
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper strategies questioned as he relies heavily on Bosnia results.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's Strategic Genius Put to Test Against Sweden and Curacao
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Backpass Assassin aims to prove drafting Ivory Coast was intentional.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Poised for Redemption as Germany Faces Ecuador
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker's quiet draft choice may finally make some noise.

pundit_takes.headline: USA Set for Late-Night Showdown Against Turkey in Inglewood
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper's roster depth questioned as he relies on USA to deliver.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner, you're the favorite with a 3-game losing streak? That's rich.
Rafe drafted three World Cup no-shows and still ranks 4th. Pure chaos.
Rafe and Gayden are tied in their head-to-head; family dinners are awkward.
Sim's favorite pick got wrecked by a 15-year-old's random dart throws. Classic.
